title: Dwayne Johnson Slammed as a ‘Coward’ for Staying Quiet on Politics; ‘Star Trek’ Icon George Takei Fires Back: ‘Silence Is Complicity’
content:
ByZack Sharf

Digital News Director

Dwayne Johnsonis catching flak on social media after he toldEsquire magazineearlier this month that he will no longer speak publicly on politics. “Star Trek” iconGeorge Takeiis one of the Hollywood voices criticizing Johnson’s stance, writingon Threads: “Silence is complicity.”

“So disappointing to find out he is such a coward,”replied“Stand By Me” actorWil Wheaton.

Johnson, who is currently embarking on a global press tour for Disney live-action “Moana” film, notably toldFox Newsin 2024 that he regretted endorsing Joe Biden for president in 2020 because it caused division amongst his fans, adding at the time: “My goal is to bring this country together. I believe in that. There will be no endorsement. At this level of influence, I will keep my politics to myself. It is between me and the ballot box.”

The blockbuster actor expanded on his newfound aversion to political talk by telling Esquire this month: “What I have learned through experience is that I need to keep — need, not want — the main thing. And the main thing for me, the thing that in the morning I swing my legs out of bed and I run towards, is creating. It’s art. It’s storytelling. I’ve learned I’m going to keep my politics to myself. There are moments when, hey, there’s nothing we can’t talk about. If I’m wrong, I’ll tell you I’m wrong. Or if I feel like I got a leg up and this is the right way to go, I’ll share it with you. Politics is omnipresent and it’s forever. I don’t like it. I hate it at times. I hate the slinging. I hate all the bullshit that comes with it.”

Jennifer Lawrence faced similar scrutiny last fall on the press tour for “Die My Love” when she spoke toThe New York Timesabout her newfound resistance to talking to the press about politics. The Oscar winner was outspoken againstDonald Trumpduring his first administration as U.S. president, notablywriting an op-edafter Election Day in which she declared that Trump’s victory meant “the only people that feel safe [in America], that their rights are recognized and respected, are white men.”

When asked about her thoughts on speaking out now regarding Trump and politics, Lawrence answered: “I don’t really know if I should. The first Trump administration was so wild and just how can we let this stand? I felt like I was running around like a chicken with my head cut off. But as we’ve learned, election after election, celebrities do not make a difference whatsoever on who people vote for. So then what am I doing? I’m just sharing my opinion on something that’s going to add fuel to a fire that’s ripping the country apart. We are so divided.”

“I think I’m in a complicated recalibration because I’m also an artist,” Lawrence continued. “With this temperature and the way things can turn out, I don’t want to start turning people off to films and to art that could change consciousness or change the world because they don’t like my political opinions. I want to protect my craft so that you can still get lost in what I’m doing. And if I can’t say something that’s going to speak to some kind of peace or lowering the temperature or some sort of solution, I don’t want to be a part of the problem. I don’t want to make the problem worse… You watch these actors’ faces who have had incredible careers and made incredible contributions and then one half of the internet doesn’t want to see their face anymore. I get so upset for those people and it feels so wrong.”

Lawrence noted thats she’s able to get across her political beliefs via her work, such as the movies she’s making at her production company. These projects include “Bread and Roses,” which follows three women in Afghanistan amid the effects of the 2021 Taliban offensive, and the abortion documentary “Zurawski v Texas.”

“I try to express my politics through my work,” she noted. “A lot of movies coming out from my production company are expressions of the political landscape and that’s how I feel like I can be helpful.”
title_zh: 德維恩·強森(Dwayne Johnson)因對政治保持沉默而被譴責為「懦夫」；《星際迷航》(Star Trek)的偶像喬治·竹井(George Takei)反擊道：「沉默就是共謀。」
content_zh:
作者：扎克·沙夫(Zack Sharf)

數位新聞總監

德維恩·約翰遜(Dwayne Johnson)在社交媒體上受到批評，因為他本月早些時候告訴《時尚先生》(Esquire)雜誌，他將不再公開談論政治。好萊塢影星喬治·竹井(George Takei)是批評約翰遜立場的聲音之一，他在Threads上寫道：“沉默就是共謀。”

“發現他這麼懦弱真讓人失望，”《伴我同行》(Stand By Me)的演員威爾·惠頓(Wil Wheaton)回應道。

約翰遜目前正在為迪士尼真人版《海洋奇緣》(Moana)進行全球宣傳，他在2024年對福克斯新聞(Fox News)表示，他後悔在2020年支持喬·拜登(Joe Biden)競選總統，因為這在他的粉絲中造成了分歧，他當時補充道：“我的目標是讓這個國家團結起來。我相信這一點。將不會有任何支持。在這種影響力的層面上，我會將我的政治觀保持私密。這是我和投票箱之間的事。”

這位票房巨星在本月告訴《時尚先生》時擴展了他對政治話題的新厭惡：“我從經驗中學到的是，我需要保持——需要，而不是想要——主要的事情。對我來說，主要的事情，就是早上我從床上起來，朝著創造奔跑的方向走。這是藝術。這是講故事。我已經學會了，我會將我的政治觀保持私密。有時候，嘿，沒有什麼是我們不能談的。如果我錯了，我會告訴你我錯了。或者如果我覺得我有優勢，這是正確的方向，我會和你分享。政治是無所不在的，永遠存在。我不喜歡它。有時我恨它。我厭惡這種互相攻擊。我厭惡與之相關的所有廢話。”

珍妮佛·勞倫斯(Jennifer Lawrence)在去年秋季為《死去我愛》(Die My Love)的宣傳中也面臨類似的審視，當時她對《紐約時報》(The New York Times)談到了她對於不再與媒體談論政治的抵抗。這位奧斯卡獲獎者在唐納德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的第一任總統任期內直言不諱，特別是在選舉日後撰寫了一篇專欄，聲明特朗普的勝利意味著“在美國，唯一感到安全、其權利被認可和尊重的人，是白人男性。”

當被問及她現在對於特朗普和政治發聲的看法時，勞倫斯回答：“我真的不知道我是否應該這樣做。第一任特朗普政府是如此瘋狂，我們怎麼能讓這種情況持續下去？我感覺自己就像一隻無頭雞在四處亂跑。但正如我們所學到的，選舉一次又一次，名人對於人們投票的影響根本沒有任何作用。所以那我在做什麼？我只是在分享我的意見，這會為撕裂國家的火焰添柴加火。我們是如此分裂。”

“我覺得我正在經歷一個複雜的重新調整，因為我也是一位藝術家，”勞倫斯(Jennifer Lawrence)繼續說道。“在這種氣氛和事情可能發展的方式下，我不想讓人們因為不喜歡我的政治觀點而對電影和藝術產生反感，這些藝術可能改變意識或改變世界。我想保護我的創作，讓你們仍然能夠沉浸在我所做的事情中。如果我無法說出一些能夠傳達和平、降低緊張或某種解決方案的話，我不想成為問題的一部分。我不想讓問題變得更糟……你看到那些擁有驚人職業生涯並做出卓越貢獻的演員的臉孔，然後網路的一半人不想再看到他們的臉。我為這些人感到非常難過，這感覺是如此不對。”

勞倫斯提到，她能夠通過她的作品傳達她的政治信念，例如她在製作公司拍攝的電影。這些項目包括《麵包與玫瑰》(Bread and Roses)，講述三位在2021年塔利班攻勢影響下的阿富汗女性，以及關於墮胎的紀錄片《祖拉夫斯基訴德克薩斯州》(Zurawski v Texas)。

“我試著通過我的作品表達我的政治觀點，”她指出。“我製作公司的許多電影都是對政治現狀的表達，我覺得這是我能夠有所幫助的方式。”
summary_zh:

數位新聞總監
